revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神
素盞鳴尊
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）}} '''大邱神社'''（だいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代 (朝鮮)|日本統治時代]]の[[朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (植民地時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱府]]（現 [[大韓民国]][[慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市]]）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）[[11月3日]]に創建され、韓国併合後の[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）[[4月22日]]に正式な神社になった。[[1937年]]（[[昭和]]12年）[[5月15日]]、[[平壌神社]]とともに国幣小社に列格した。[[1945年]]の[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[日本]]の敗戦の後、廃座された。 [[Category:朝鮮の神社|たいきゆうしんしや]] [[Category:国幣小社|たいきゆうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神
素盞鳴尊
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）}} '''大邱神社'''（だいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代 (朝鮮)|日本統治時代]]の[[朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱府]]（現 [[大韓民国]][[慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市]]）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）[[11月3日]]に創建され、韓国併合後の[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）[[4月22日]]に正式な神社になった。[[1937年]]（[[昭和]]12年）[[5月15日]]、[[平壌神社]]とともに国幣小社に列格した。[[1945年]]の[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[日本]]の敗戦の後、廃座された。 [[Category:朝鮮の神社|たいきゆうしんしや]] [[Category:国幣小社|たいきゆうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神
素盞鳴尊
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）}} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱府]]（現 [[大韓民国]][[大邱広域市]]）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）[[11月3日]]に創建され、[[韓国併合]]後の[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）[[4月22日]]に正式な神社になった。[[1937年]]（[[昭和]]12年）[[5月15日]]、[[平壌神社]]とともに国幣小社に列格した。[[1945年]]の[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[大日本帝国|日本]]の敗戦の後、廃座された。 {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神
素盞鳴尊
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）}} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市|大邱府]]（現・[[大韓民国]]大邱広域市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）[[11月3日]]に創建され、[[韓国併合]]後の[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）[[4月22日]]に正式な神社になった。[[1937年]]（[[昭和]]12年）[[5月15日]]、[[平安南道 (日本統治時代)|平安南道]][[平壌|平壌府]]の[[平壌神社]]とともに国幣小社に列格した。[[1945年]]の[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[大日本帝国|日本]]の敗戦の後、廃座された。 {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神
素盞鳴尊
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）}} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市|大邱府]]（現・[[大韓民国]]大邱広域市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は[[天照大神]]・[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]であった。 ==歴史== [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）[[11月3日]]に創建され、[[韓国併合]]後の[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）[[4月22日]]に正式な神社になった。[[1937年]]（[[昭和]]12年）[[5月15日]]、[[平安南道 (日本統治時代)|平安南道]][[平壌|平壌府]]の[[平壌神社]]とともに国幣小社に列格した。[[1945年]]の[[第二次世界大戦]]の[[大日本帝国|日本]]の敗戦の後、廃座された。 {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない建築物]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）| 例祭=10月16日}} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市|大邱府]]（現・[[大韓民国]]大邱広域市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は主神が[[天照大神]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]で、ほかに[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]が配祀されていた。 ==歴史== [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）11月3日、府内の達城山に神殿を造営し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（大邱神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.B02031402700 （外務省外交史料館）「大邱神社寺院教会」 『慶北要覧』（大邱新聞社、1910年）。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、大邱神社と改称し、[[1915年]]（大正4年）に神殿を改築するとともに参殿を新築して神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[4月22日]]、天照大神を祭神とする大邱神社の創立が許可された「神社創立許可」 朝鮮総督府官報第1116号（大正5年4月26日） 390頁。 [[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]、道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定された朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁。同年[[12月7日]]には国魂大神の増祀が許可され、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）[[5月15日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第316号 朝鮮総督府官報号外（昭和12年5月15日） 1頁。 [[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない建築物]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）| 例祭=10月16日}} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市|大邱府]]（現・[[大韓民国]]大邱広域市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は主神が[[天照大神]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]で、ほかに[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]が配祀されていた。 ==歴史== [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）11月3日、府内の達城山に神殿を造営し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（大邱神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.B02031402700 （外務省外交史料館）「大邱神社寺院教会」 『慶北要覧』（大邱新聞社、1910年）。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、大邱神社と改称し、[[1915年]]（大正4年）に神殿を改築するとともに参殿を新築して神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[4月22日]]、天照大神を祭神とする大邱神社の創立が許可された「神社創立許可」 朝鮮総督府官報第1116号（大正5年4月26日） 390頁。 [[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]、道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定された朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁。同年[[12月7日]]には国魂大神の増祀が許可され、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）[[5月15日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第316号 朝鮮総督府官報号外（昭和12年5月15日） 1頁。 [[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 ==脚注== {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない韓国の建築物]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地=朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神

{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地={{flagicon|JPN1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神

revision.text:
{{神社| 名称=大邱神社| 所在地={{flagicon|JPN1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町| 祭神=天照大神
国魂大神| 社格=国幣小社| 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）| 例祭=10月16日}} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市|大邱府]]（現・[[大韓民国]]大邱広域市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は主神が[[天照大神]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]で、ほかに[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]が配祀されていた。 == 歴史 == [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）11月3日、府内の達城山に神殿を造営し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（大邱神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.B02031402700 （外務省外交史料館）「大邱神社寺院教会」 『慶北要覧』（大邱新聞社、1910年）。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、大邱神社と改称し、[[1915年]]（大正4年）に神殿を改築するとともに参殿を新築して神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[4月22日]]、天照大神を祭神とする大邱神社の創立が許可された「神社創立許可」 朝鮮総督府官報第1116号（大正5年4月26日） 390頁。 [[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]、道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定された朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁。同年[[12月7日]]には国魂大神の増祀が許可され、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）[[5月15日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第316号 朝鮮総督府官報号外（昭和12年5月15日） 1頁。 [[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない韓国の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社|廃たいきゆうしんしや]] {{shinto-stub}}

{{神社 | 名称=大邱神社 | 所在地={{flagicon|JPN1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 | 祭神=天照大神
国魂大神 | 社格=国幣小社 | 創建=[[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年） | 例祭=10月16日 }} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市|大邱府]]（現・[[大韓民国]]大邱広域市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は主神が[[天照大神]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]で、ほかに[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]が配祀されていた。 == 歴史 == [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）11月3日、府内の達城山に神殿を造営し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（大邱神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.B02031402700 （外務省外交史料館）「大邱神社寺院教会」 『慶北要覧』（大邱新聞社、1910年）。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、大邱神社と改称し、[[1915年]]（大正4年）に神殿を改築するとともに参殿を新築して神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[4月22日]]、天照大神を祭神とする大邱神社の創立が許可された「神社創立許可」 朝鮮総督府官報第1116号（大正5年4月26日） 390頁。 [[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]、道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定された朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁。同年[[12月7日]]には国魂大神の増祀が許可され、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）[[5月15日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第316号 朝鮮総督府官報号外（昭和12年5月15日） 1頁。 [[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない韓国の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社|廃たいきゆうしんしや]]

revision.comment: Created by translating the page "[[:ja:Special:Redirect/revision/94813070|大邱神社]]"
revision.text:
{{神社|画像=[[File:Taikyu Shrine.jpg|250px|center]]|名称=大邱神社|所在地={{flagicon|JPN1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町|祭神=天照大神
国魂大神|社格=国幣小社|創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年）|例祭=10月15日}}'''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945 内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 . == Sources == [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]]

Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 == References == [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]]

{{神社 | 画像=[[File:Taikyū-jinja.jpg|250px|center]] | 名称=大邱神社 | 所在地={{flagicon|JPN1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 | 祭神=天照大神
国魂大神 | 社格=国幣小社 | 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年） | 例祭=10月15日 }} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市|大邱府]]（現・[[大韓民国]]大邱広域市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は主神が[[天照大神]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]で、ほかに[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]が配祀されていた。 == 歴史 == [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）11月3日、府内の達城山に神殿を造営し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（大邱神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.B02031402700 （外務省外交史料館）「大邱神社寺院教会」 『慶北要覧』（大邱新聞社、1910年）。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、大邱神社と改称し、[[1915年]]（大正4年）に神殿を改築するとともに参殿を新築して神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[4月22日]]、天照大神を祭神とする大邱神社の創立が許可された「神社創立許可」 朝鮮総督府官報第1116号（大正5年4月26日） 390頁。 [[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]、道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定された朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁。同年[[12月7日]]には国魂大神の増祀が許可され、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）[[5月15日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第316号 朝鮮総督府官報号外（昭和12年5月15日） 1頁。 [[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない韓国の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社|廃たいきゆうしんしや]]

{{infobox religious building |image=Taikyū-jinja.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |deity=天照大神
国魂大神 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 == References == [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]]

The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |deity=天照大神
国魂大神 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== {{Shrine | Image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | Name=Taikyū Shrine | Location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | Deities={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitsuhikonomikoto|ja|国魂大神}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | Annual Festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Taikyū-bu|ja|大邱府}}, {{ill|Gyeongsangbuk-do|ja|慶尚北道}} (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese rule of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|社格}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitsuhikonomikoto|ja|国魂大神}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitsuhikonomikoto|ja|国魂大神}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |deity=天照大神
国魂大神 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== {{Shrine | Image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | Name=Taikyū Shrine | Location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | Deities={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitsuhikonomikoto|ja|国魂大神}} | Shrine Rank={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | Annual Festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Taikyū-bu|ja|大邱府}}, {{ill|Gyeongsangbuk-do|ja|慶尚北道}} (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|社格}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitsuhikonomikoto|ja|国魂大神}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitsuhikonomikoto|ja|国魂大神}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] ==References== {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | Image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | Name=Taikyū Shrine | Location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Taikyū-bu|ja|大邱府}}, {{ill|Gyeongsangbuk-do|ja|慶尚北道}} (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|社格}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Https://zh.wikipedia.org/wiki/]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | Image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | Location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Taikyū-bu|ja|大邱府}}, {{ill|North Gyeongsang Province|ja|慶尚北道}} (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|社格}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | Image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | Location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|社格}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq---qq]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:bump-01-18]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]][[Category:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:Qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:Subst:NAMESPACE]] [[Category:q--q]] [[Category:q--q]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taikyu The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings revision.text:
{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[ja:大邱神社]] {{Reflist}} [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category: tier 6]][[Category: tier 6]]
