On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:大邱神社]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taikyu Shrine}}

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市|12=draft}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国|12=draft}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|12=draft}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=draft}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|12=draft}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=draft}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] }} [[ja:大邱神社]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taikyu Shrine}}

{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市|12=draft}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国|12=draft}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|12=draft}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=draft}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=draft}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Taikyu Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[ja:大邱神社]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taikyu Shrine}}

In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft|12=draft}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=draft|12=draft}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft|12=draft}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] [[Category:tab went inactive so I need to add another category]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taikyu Shrine}}

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft|12=draft}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft|12=draft}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|12=draft|12=draft}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国|12=draft|12=draft}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|12=draft|12=draft}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=draft|12=draft}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft|12=draft}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft|12=draft}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=draft|12=draft}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|12=draft|12=draft}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=draft|12=draft}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|12=draft|12=draft}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=draft|12=draft}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=draft|12=draft}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=draft|12=draft}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=draft|12=draft}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=draft|12=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taikyu Shrine}}

On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taikyu Shrine}}

revision.comment: 17 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:大邱神社 up to rev 99155365
revision.text:
{{神社 | 画像=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | 名称=大邱神社 | 所在地={{flagicon|JPN1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 | 祭神=天照大神
国魂大神 | 社格=国幣小社 | 創建=[[1916年]]（[[大正]]5年） | 例祭=10月15日 }} '''大邱神社'''（たいきゅうじんじゃ）は、[[日本統治時代の朝鮮|朝鮮]][[慶尚北道 (日本統治時代)|慶尚北道]][[大邱広域市|大邱府]]（現・[[大韓民国]]大邱広域市）にあった[[神社]]である。旧[[社格]]は[[近代社格制度|国幣小社]]。 [[祭神]]は主神が[[天照大神]]・[[国魂|国魂大神]]で、ほかに[[スサノオ|素盞鳴尊]]が配祀されていた。 == 歴史 == [[1906年]]（[[明治]]39年）11月3日、府内の達城山に神殿を造営し皇大神宮の遙拝所とした「神社明細帳（大邱神社）」 JACAR（アジア歴史資料センター） Ref.B02031402700 （外務省外交史料館）「大邱神社寺院教会」 『慶北要覧』（大邱新聞社、1910年）。 [[1913年]]（[[大正]]2年）、大邱神社と改称し、[[1915年]]（大正4年）に神殿を改築するとともに参殿を新築して神社創立を出願し、神社寺院規則（大正4年朝鮮総督府令第82号）に基づいて、[[1916年]]（大正5年）[[4月22日]]、天照大神を祭神とする大邱神社の創立が許可された「神社創立許可」 朝鮮総督府官報第1116号（大正5年4月26日） 390頁。 [[1936年]]（昭和11年）[[8月11日]]、道供進社（道より神饌幣帛料を供進すべき神社）に指定された朝鮮総督府告示第440号 朝鮮総督府官報第2874号（昭和11年8月11日） 83頁。同年[[12月7日]]には国魂大神の増祀が許可され、[[1937年]]（昭和12年）[[5月15日]]、国幣小社に列格された朝鮮総督府告示第316号 朝鮮総督府官報号外（昭和12年5月15日） 1頁。 [[大日本帝国|日本]]の[[第二次世界大戦]][[敗戦]]に伴い、[[1945年]]（昭和20年）[[11月17日]]に廃止された内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20年11月22日） 1頁。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たいきゆうしんしや}} [[Category:朝鮮の神社]] [[Category:国幣小社]] [[Category:現存しない韓国の建築物]] [[Category:神明神社|廃たいきゆうしんしや]]

{{神社 | 画像=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | 名称=大邱神社 | 所在地={{flagicon|JPN1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 | 祭神=天照大神

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}

Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Taikyu Shrine}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}}

On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity={{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ({{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now {{ill|Daegu|ja|大邱広域市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|South Korea|ja|大韓民国|qq=draft|qq=draft}}), during the {{ill|Japanese occupation of Korea|ja|日本統治時代の朝鮮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhesha]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]]

The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}}, with {{ill|Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ({{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu Ōmikami|ja|天照大神|1=Amaterasu|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu Ōmikami|13=User:Immanuelle/Amaterasu|qq=}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|Second World War|ja|第二次世界大戦|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Second World War|qq=}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Taikyu Shrine]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]]

On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]]

{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}} | Foundation=[[1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]]), during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|qq=}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Normdaten}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[en:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]]

On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]]

The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]]

revision.comment: Bot: convert local link → enwiki interwiki
revision.text:
{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}} | Foundation=[[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[Daegu]] during the [[Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]]), during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Defunct buildings in South Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines dedicated to Amaterasu]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]]

On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]]

revision.text:
{{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | image=[[File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg|250px|center]] | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}} | Foundation=[[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[Shinto Shrine]] in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]] during the [[:en:Japanese occupation of Korea|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]]), during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]]

The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]]

On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]]

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11439410]] Wikidata name: Taikyu Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}} | Foundation=[[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]] during the [[:en:Japanese occupation of Korea|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]]), during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ|1=Type|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2463013}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[sn:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[sn:aikyu Shrine]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}}

{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}} | Foundation=[[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]] during the [[:en:Japanese occupation of Korea|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]]), during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ|1=Type|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2463013}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|qq=|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}}

The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}}
