On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

[[ja:大邱神社]][[simple:Daegu Shrine]][[zh:大邱神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11439410]] Wikidata name: Taikyu Jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:41, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image=File:Taikyū-jinja torii.jpg |native_name=大邱神社 |native_name_lang=ja |location={{flagicon|JPN|1889}}大日本帝国朝鮮慶尚北道大邱府城西町 |consecration_year=1916 |date_demolished=November 17, 1945 | name=Taikyū Shrine | location={{flagicon|JPN1889}} Empire of Japan, Korea, Gyeongsangbuk-do, Taikyū-bu, Seisei Town | deity=[[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]]
{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}} | Foundation=[[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]] during the [[:en:Japanese occupation of Korea|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]]), during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ|1=Type|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2463013}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]][[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]]

{{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}} | Foundation=[[:en:1916|1916]] ([[:en:Taishō era|Taishō]] 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]] during the [[:en:Japanese occupation of Korea|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]]), during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ|1=Type|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2463013}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] }} [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]][[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| }} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:North_Gyeongsang_Province|North Gyeongsang Province]] (now [[:en:Daegu|Daegu]], [[:en:South Korea|South Korea]]), during the [[:en:Korea under Japanese rule|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ|1=Type|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2463013}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. The enshrined deities were primarily [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]] and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, [[:en:1906|1906]] ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In [[:en:1913|1913]] (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In [[:en:1915|1915]] (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, [[:en:1916|1916]] (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to [[:en:Amaterasu|Amaterasu Ōmikami]], was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, [[:en:1936|1936]] (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, [[:en:1937|1937]] (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the [[:en:World War II|Second World War]], the shrine was abolished on November 17, [[:en:1945|1945]] (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu}}, {{ill|North_Gyeongsang_Province|en|North_Gyeongsang_Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}} (now {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea}}), during the {{ill|Korea under Japanese rule|en|Korea under Japanese rule|WD=Q503585|lt=Japanese occupation of Korea}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}} (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936}} (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} | type={{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}} | Foundation={{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} ({{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō}} 5) | festival=October 15 }} '''Taikyu Jinja''' was {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu}} during the [[:en:Japanese occupation of Korea|Japanese occupation of Korea]]. The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu}}, {{ill|North_Gyeongsang_Province|en|North_Gyeongsang_Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province}} (now {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea}}), during the {{ill|Korea under Japanese rule|en|Korea under Japanese rule|WD=Q503585|lt=Japanese occupation of Korea}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ|1=Type|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2463013}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913}} (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915}} (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916}} (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936}} (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=1937}} (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Kokuhesha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945}} (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu|1=Daegu}}, {{ill|North_Gyeongsang_Province|en|North_Gyeongsang_Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province|1=North Gyeongsang Province}} (now {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu|1=Daegu}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}}), during the {{ill|Korea under Japanese rule|en|Korea under Japanese rule|WD=Q503585|lt=Japanese occupation of Korea|1=Korea under Japanese rule}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ|1=Type|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2463013}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906|1=1906}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War|1=World War II}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}} [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with simple english wikipedia interwikis]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:pages]]

The main deities enshrined here were [[Amaterasu|Amaterasu Omikami]] and [[Kunitama|Kunitama Omikami]], and [[Susanoo-no-Mikoto|Susanoo no Mikoto]] was also enshrined. Japan's defeat in World War II led to the shrine's abolition on November 17, 1945.内務省告示第264号 官報第5660号（昭和20th year 11月22日） 1頁 ==Overview== '''Taikyū Shrine''' (大邱神社) was a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu|1=Daegu}}, {{ill|North_Gyeongsang_Province|en|North_Gyeongsang_Province|WD=Q41154|lt=North Gyeongsang Province|1=North Gyeongsang Province}} (now {{ill|Daegu|en|Daegu|WD=Q20927|lt=Daegu|1=Daegu}}, {{ill|South Korea|en|South Korea|WD=Q884|lt=South Korea|1=South Korea}}), during the {{ill|Korea under Japanese rule|en|Korea under Japanese rule|WD=Q503585|lt=Japanese occupation of Korea|1=Korea under Japanese rule}}. The shrine's former {{ill|shakaku|ja|type|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shakaku|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タイプ|1=Type|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2463013}} was {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}. The enshrined deities were primarily {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} and {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, with {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞鳴尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}} also worshipped. == History == On November 3, {{ill|1906|en|1906|WD=Q2049|lt=1906|1=1906}} ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 39), a shrine building was constructed on Mount Dalseong within the city as a place of worship for the {{ill|Ise Grand Shrine|ja|皇大神宮|1=Kōtai Jingū|2=ja|3=皇大神宮|4=zh|5=皇大神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ise Grand Shrine|13=User:Immanuelle/Kōtai Jingū|comment=enwiki is a redirect}}."Shrine Register (Taikyū Shrine)" JACAR (Japan Center for Asian Historical Records) Ref.B02031402700 (Ministry of Foreign Affairs Diplomatic Archives)"Taikyū Shrine Temples and Churches," *Gyeongbuk Yōran* (Taikyū Shinbunsha, 1910) In {{ill|1913|en|1913|WD=Q2080|lt=1913|1=1913}} (Taishō 2), it was renamed Taikyū Shrine. In {{ill|1915|en|1915|WD=Q2084|lt=1915|1=1915}} (Taishō 4), the shrine building was reconstructed, and an application was submitted to formally establish the shrine. On April 22, {{ill|1916|en|1916|WD=Q2087|lt=1916|1=1916}} (Taishō 5), the shrine's establishment, dedicated to {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|WD=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, was approved based on the {{ill|Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja|神社寺院規則|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Regulations on Shrines and Temples|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} issued by the {{ill|Government-General of Korea|ja|朝鮮総督府|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Government-General of Korea|qq=}}."Shrine Establishment Approval," Government-General of Korea Gazette No. 1116 (April 26, 1916), p. 390 On August 11, {{ill|1936|en|1936|WD=Q18649|lt=1936|1=1936}} (Shōwa 11), the shrine was designated as a {{ill|Dō Kyōshinsha|ja|道供進社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dō Kyōshinsha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (a shrine receiving offerings and ceremonial funding from the province).Government-General of Korea Notification No. 440, Gazette No. 2874 (August 11, 1936), p. 83 Later, on December 7 of the same year, the enshrinement of {{ill|Kunitama|ja|国魂大神|Kunitama=Kunitama|lt=Kunitama Ōkami|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kunitama|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was permitted. On May 15, {{ill|1937|en|1937|WD=Q18647|lt=1937|1=1937}} (Shōwa 12), Taikyū Shrine was promoted to the Rank of {{ill|Kokuhesha|ja|国幣小社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuhesha|1=Q9685496|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q9685496}}.Government-General of Korea Notification No. 316, Gazette Special Issue (May 15, 1937), p. 1 Following Japan's defeat in the {{ill|World War II|en|World War II|WD=Q362|lt=Second World War|1=World War II}}, the shrine was abolished on November 17, {{ill|1945|en|1945|WD=Q5240|lt=1945|1=1945}} (Shōwa 20).Ministry of Home Affairs Notification No. 264, Official Gazette No. 5660 (November 22, 1945), p. 1 == References == {{Reflist}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{Reflist}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} {{translated page|ja|大邱神社|version=99155365|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[simple:Taikyu Shrine]] [[ja:大邱神社]] [[zh:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[ja:%E5%A4%A7%E9%82%B1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE]] [[zh:大邱神社]] [[zh:大邱神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11439410}} [[Category:Former buildings and structures in South Korea]] [[Category:Kokuhei Shōsha]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:Shinmei shrines]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Korea]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in the Japanese colonial empire]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
